svg.text: Prob bind: apply f per outcome, weight by pa, merge — law of total probability

g.title: Apply f(a1): get the conditional distribution Prob b given A = a1
Apply f(a2): get the conditional distribution Prob b given A = a2
Scale: each (b, pb) in sub1 gets weight pa1 * pb — P(B=b | A=a1) * P(A=a1)
Scale: each (b, pb) in sub2 gets weight pa2 * pb — P(B=b | A=a2) * P(A=a2)
Concatenate: w1 ++ w2 (and repeat for all other branches). Result is the marginal distribution P(B).
Prob a = [(a, p)] — each (a, pa) is an outcome with probability pa. Repeats for every element (∀ a).
(a ⟶ Prob b) — conditional distribution: given each outcome a, returns a distribution over b
P(A = a1) — probability of outcome a1
P(A = a2) — probability of outcome a2
Prob b — conditional distribution for branch a1
Prob b — conditional distribution for branch a2
[(b, pa1·pb)] — weighted outcomes for branch a1
[(b, pa2·pb)] — weighted outcomes for branch a2
Prob b — the marginal distribution: P(B=b) = Σ_a P(B=b|A=a)·P(A=a)
